generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance PrimaryMotorShaftEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft of the MABI MAX 100 robotic arm, providing primary positional feedback for motion control. Facilitates backlash reduction and positional uncertainty mitigation through direct shaft coupling."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorShaftPositionData
]

instance SecondaryCycloidalGearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder positioned behind the cycloidal gearbox of the MABI MAX 100 robotic arm, providing secondary positional feedback to complement primary encoder data. Strategically placed to improve motion accuracy through gearbox backlash compensation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorShaftPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PrimaryMotorShaftEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryCycloidalGearboxEncoder
]

instance CombinedPositionTelemetry : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftPositionData, GearboxPositionData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MultiPhysicsFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Multi-physics aware model combining mechanical and electrical simulations to analyze joint friction in the MABI MAX 100 robotic system. Implemented using MathWorks Simulink for behavior description and C++ for detailed friction analysis algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEngine, FrictionAnalysisAlgorithm
]

instance SysMLComponentConnectorDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) representing component-connector relationships for the MABI MAX 100 robotic arm's mechanical assembly, including motor, gearbox, and joint interfaces. Used for structural modeling and digital twin architecture description."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance ContextualDataTraceRepository : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated repository of contextual data traces including sensor telemetry (position, torque, environmental parameters) from the MABI MAX 100 robotic system. Serves as the passive data foundation for friction identification and motion accuracy analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftPositionData, GearboxPositionData, CombinedPositionTelemetry
]

instance FrictionCoefficientMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived friction-related metrics (e.g., coefficient of friction, friction torque) calculated from processed sensor data traces. Used for motion accuracy assessment and diagnostic purposes without explicit confidence thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CombinedPositionTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisAlgorithm
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTDataStreamingProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation enabling real-time, one-way data streaming from physical sensors (PrimaryMotorShaftEncoder, SecondaryCycloidalGearboxEncoder) to the Digital Twin's computational models. Supports low-latency transmission of positional telemetry data."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataAggregationService, FrictionIdentificationService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine executing the MultiPhysicsFrictionModel to analyze robotic joint dynamics and friction characteristics. Provides behavioral simulation capabilities for motion accuracy assessment."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService
]

instance FrictionAnalysisAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom C++/Java algorithm implementing multi-physics aware friction detection logic. Processes contextual data traces to identify mechanical friction anomalies in the gearbox/motor assembly for motion accuracy improvement."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService
]

instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool (e.g., IBM Rational Rhapsody) used to create and maintain the SysMLComponentConnectorDiagram representing the robotic system's component-connector architecture. Enables hierarchical modeling of physical components."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboardService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive monitoring service aggregating and abstracting contextual data traces (positional telemetry, environmental parameters) from the MABI MAX 100 robotic system via MQTT protocol. Enables real-time state tracking of physical components without bidirectional feedback."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionStateInsight, DataTraceRepositoryInsight
]

instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Specialized service analyzing processed sensor data to detect mechanical friction anomalies in the robotic joint assembly. Provides friction-related metrics for motion accuracy improvement without autonomous system adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCoefficientInsight, MotionAccuracyDiagnosticInsight
]

instance VisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard service presenting aggregated data and derived insights (e.g., friction coefficients, motion state) to manufacturing engineers and maintenance technicians. Enables manual decision-making through data visualization."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizedFrictionMetricsInsight, OperationalStateInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance MotionStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous, real-time insight into the operational state of the MABI MAX 100 robotic arm's mechanical components based on aggregated positional telemetry data. Supports passive monitoring of system health without predictive capabilities."
]

instance DataTraceRepositoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured repository of contextual data traces enabling post-processing analysis of the robotic system's operational history. Serves as the foundation for friction identification and diagnostic services."
]

instance FrictionCoefficientInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Derived insight representing calculated friction coefficients and related metrics from processed sensor data. Used by engineers to assess joint friction impact on motion accuracy without explicit confidence thresholds."
]

instance MotionAccuracyDiagnosticInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Diagnostic insight identifying mechanical friction anomalies in the robotic joint assembly that may affect motion precision. Provides purpose-driven analysis for maintenance decision support."
]

instance VisualizedFrictionMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Graphical representation of friction-related metrics (e.g., coefficient of friction, torque variations) presented through the VisualizationDashboardService for operator analysis."
]

instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualized operational state of the robotic system components including real-time and historical performance metrics accessible via the interactive dashboard."
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingRoboticsEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial manufacturing environment housing the MABI MAX 100 robotic system and its associated Digital Shadow implementation. Characterized by real-time data requirements and purpose-driven friction analysis for motion accuracy improvement."
    base:contains MABIRobotPhysicalSystem, DigitalShadowSystem
]

instance MABIRobotPhysicalSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical MABI MAX 100 serial robotic arm system including motor assemblies, cycloidal gearboxes, and positional encoders. Operates within the manufacturing environment with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller for motion control."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingRoboticsEnvironment
]

instance DigitalShadowSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow implementation for the MABI MAX 100 robotic system providing passive data aggregation and friction analysis capabilities. Comprises computational models, data repositories, and visualization services without active control functionality."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingRoboticsEnvironment
    base:contains PrimaryMotorShaftEncoder, SecondaryCycloidalGearboxEncoder,
                 MultiPhysicsFrictionModel, ContextualDataTraceRepository,
                 MQTTDataStreamingProtocol, SimulinkSimulationEngine,
                 FrictionAnalysisAlgorithm, SysMLModelingTool
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents emphasizes the need for **real-time or near-real-time synchronization** between physical and digital components, particularly when integrating with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) or latency requirements are not detailed, the context implies that **temporal granularity** must align with the operational demands of the system, often requiring **low-latency data flows** for active interpretation (e.g., via active components in Digital Twin architectures). The documents reference **heterogeneous modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java) that may introduce varying time scales for simulation or behavioral modeling, necessitating **coordinated time management** across layers (requirements, functional, logical, and physical). For **Digital Shadows**, the one-way data flow implies **asynchronous or event-driven aggregation** of contextual data traces, where temporal granularity depends on the specific use case (e.g., manufacturing) but lacks quantifiable metrics in the provided text."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration is grounded in **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies**, which leverage **architecture patterns** from **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** (e.g., ACME, AADL, MontiArc) to define **component-based architectures** with explicit **component & connector perspectives**. Integration of heterogeneous tools (e.g., SysML, Simulink, C++/Java) requires **resource management** via **model interoperability frameworks**, though no specific orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) are mentioned. System-wide coordination is implied through **state machines** (SysML v1.7) or **automata** (MontiArc) for behavioral modeling, but **real-time coordination mechanisms** (e.g., publish-subscribe, event-driven systems) are not detailed. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) suggests a **passive aggregation layer** for contextual data, while the Digital Twin itself may host **active components** for interpretation, indicating a **hybrid constellation** where passive and active elements coexist under a unified MBSE framework."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of the Digital Twin system in this context follows a **purpose-driven methodology** for Digital Shadows, structured into four sequential stages: **(1) Problem identification**, where the specific use case (e.g., joint friction analysis) is defined; **(2) Asset/model analysis**, involving the evaluation of existing physical assets and their associated models (e.g., SysML-based functional/logical architectures) to determine data requirements; **(3) Creation of models**, where calculations, simulations (e.g., multi-physics-aware models), or existing tools (e.g., Simulink for behavior modeling) are developed or integrated; and **(4) Data identification**, focusing on aggregating and abstracting contextual data traces (e.g., sensor telemetry) for the defined purpose. Transition management between stages is implicit in the methodology, emphasizing iterative refinement based on feedback from stakeholders (e.g., engineers, operators) to ensure alignment with the problem’s objectives. Lifecycle governance is not explicitly detailed in the document but implies adherence to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** principles, where abstraction layers (requirements → functional → logical → product/technical) are systematically addressed using methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** or **SysML-based architecture description languages (ADLs)** for consistency and traceability."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through structured modeling and sensor arrangement, though explicit validation/verification (V&V) methodologies are not detailed. Key considerations include:
- **Model Fidelity Requirements**:
  - **Architectural Fidelity**: Models adhere to **SysML/AADL/MontiArc standards**, ensuring consistency in component, interface, and connector representations across abstraction layers (requirements → physical).
  - **Behavioral Fidelity**: Behavioral models (state machines, Simulink) are **validated against real-world dynamics** via simulation tools, though explicit V&V protocols (e.g., test cases, formal methods) are not mentioned.
- **Validation and Verification Methods**:
  - **Implicit Sensor-Based Validation**: Uncertainty is **reduced through spatial sensor arrangement** (primary/secondary encoders) to cross-validate position data, mitigating errors from gearing or mechanical play.
  - **Model-Data Alignment**: Digital Shadows provide **contextual data traces** that are aggregated and abstracted for DT interpretation, implying a **data-driven validation loop** (though not quantified).
- **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification**:
  - **Mechanical Uncertainty**: Addressed via **redundant sensing** (dual encoders) to detect discrepancies (e.g., gear slippage, encoder drift).
  - **Model Uncertainty**: Mitigated by **layered MBSE methodologies** (RFLP, OOSEM) to ensure logical consistency across models.
- **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms**:
  - **Closed-Loop Control**: The controller uses encoder feedback to **correct deviations** in real-time, though explicit error correction algorithms (e.g., PID tuning, fault detection) are not described.
  - **Data Aggregation**: Digital Shadows aggregate traces to **identify anomalies** (e.g., sensor failures, environmental noise), though no specific quantification methods (e.g., statistical thresholds) are provided.
- **Quality Assurance Processes**:
  - **Toolchain Interoperability**: QA is implicitly enforced by **standardized ADLs (SysML, AADL)** and simulation tools (Simulink), ensuring traceability across development phases.
  - **Stakeholder Integration**: Heterogeneous perspectives are aligned via **MBSE frameworks**, though explicit QA protocols (e.g., audits, compliance checks) are not detailed.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection for this system is primarily defined by the use of **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** and **AADL (Architecture Analysis & Design Language)** to model system components, interfaces, and interconnections. The system leverages **MQTT** for streaming time-series data from physical sensors to the digital representation, with no explicit mention of additional communication protocols (e.g., OPC UA, RESTful APIs), network topology, bandwidth requirements, or latency specifications. The document does not detail reliability mechanisms, redundancy, or security protocols for network communication."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The deployment infrastructure for this system involves the use of **modeling and simulation tools** such as **MathWorks Simulink** and **general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java)** for behavioral modeling and system behavior description. The document does not specify hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise), computational/storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, deployment models, or availability/reliability assurance. No details on infrastructure deployment beyond the modeling and simulation tools are provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems primarily through **MQTT-based sensor data streams** for real-time, one-way data flows (as defined for digital shadows). Interoperability is achieved via **standardized modeling languages** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink), but no explicit mention of **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** or **ERP/MES dependencies** exists in the provided documents. The system does not describe bidirectional communication; instead, it emphasizes **passive data aggregation** from physical objects, requiring **active Digital Twin components** to process and contextualize the data. Integration patterns are limited to **heterogeneous MBSE toolchains** and **general-purpose programming languages** for behavioral modeling, with no references to enterprise system dependencies."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry standards and specifications** to ensure interoperability and compliance:

1. **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**:
   - The methodology incorporates **AAS**, a standardized framework (per **Industry 4.0 standards**) for digitalizing physical assets, enabling seamless integration with other systems and platforms.

2. **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) Standards**:
   - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** is explicitly used as the primary **modeling language** for functional and logical architecture, aligning with **INCOSE (International Council on Systems Engineering)** best practices.

3. **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)**:
   - **AADL (Avionics Architecture Description Language)** and **MontiArc** are referenced for component-based system modeling, adhering to **DO-178C (for AADL)** and **model-driven engineering** paradigms.

4. **Interoperability Standards**:
   - While not explicitly detailed, the use of **AAS and SysML** implies compliance with **ISO/IEC 42010 (Architecture Viewpoints and Stakeholders)** and **IEC 62264 (Enterprise-Control System Integration)** for system integration.

5. **Certification and Compliance Frameworks**:
   - No explicit certification requirements (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 61508) are mentioned, but the structured MBSE approach aligns with **regulatory-compliant engineering practices**.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in **Document [42]** incorporates **safety-critical operational considerations** within the automation pyramid, particularly for **injection molding processes**, where **digital shadows** enable real-time monitoring and traceability of production states. While explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., RBAC, OAuth) or **ISO/IEC 27001** compliance are not mentioned, the system emphasizes **safety through operational constraints**: Document [44] references the **MABI robotic arm**, which includes a **safety cell** restricting the maximum working range to **[-90°, 90°]** to prevent collisions, aligning with **industrial safety standards** (e.g., **ISO 10218-1** for robotic safety, though not explicitly cited). Document [45] discusses **friction modeling for robot dynamics**, which inherently supports **fail-safe mechanisms** by enabling predictive control to avoid mechanical failures. However, no **formal risk assessment frameworks** (e.g., **FMEA**) or **safety-critical system certifications** (e.g., **IEC 61508**) are detailed in the provided texts. **Threat mitigation** is implied through **data traceability** (as per [42]), but no encryption protocols (e.g., **TLS 1.3**) or **access control mechanisms** (e.g., **role-based access control**) are specified. **Document [46]** and **[47]** reference the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**, which mandates **semantic interoperability** but does not address security governance beyond **data modeling standards** (e.g., **ISO/IEC 11179**)."
]
